title: Xây dựng một ngăn xếp!
summary: COS10004 Bài 4.3 – Xây dựng ngăn xếp trong hệ thống máy tính. Cài đặt stack bằng phần cứng.
content_html:
COS10004 Computer Systems
               Lecture 4.3:  Let’s build a stack!







Dr Chris McCarthy

             STACKS
             >   Random access memory requires knowing the address of
                 every byte/word you want to access
             >   Hardware stacks created out of dedicated shift registers
             >   So let’s build one!


















2

LET'S BUILD A STACK...
>   Start with a bi-directional shift register...
>   1. Start off with 4 D flip-flops:























                                                                                 3

2. ADD THE STANDARD SIGNALS
>  Clock



























                                                               4

>   CLR (resets flip flops when up in Logisim)



























                                                                              5

ADD THE SERIAL INPUT (D)
>  Data is the serial input (SI)



























                                                                         6

CONNECT EACH Q OUTPUT TO THE NEXT DATA INPUT






























                                                          7

ADD SOME LEDS TO SEE THE PARALLEL OUTPUT / REGISTER
STATE
>  We can now modulate the Data input and see the on state
   propagate through the register (left to right).

























                                                                     8

THIS CIRCUIT WILL DO MOST THINGS WE NEED:
>  To get serial out, record the state of the right-most LED.
>  To get parallel out, feed each LED into a register (latch)
   and stop the clock when the conversion is complete.
>  To do parallel input, OR each D input with the state of a
   register and start the clock.










          but it won't go backwards...
                                                                           9

  HOW DO WE MAKE IT GO BACKWARDS?

>  Wire-up the cascade backwards
>  Data goes in the far end, each Q outputs to the D input of
   the previous Flip-Flop.





















                                                                       10

   HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
   >   Remember those controlled logic gates in Week 2 ?







Select this
AND gate
 (Ci low)

Select this
 OR gate
 (Ci high)



                                                                                11

HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
>  More specifically, we need to determine from which
   direction each Flip Flop will receive its input.
























                                                                      12

HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
>  A controlled gate (remember Week 2!).


























                                                                13

      PROGRAMMABLE WIRING

      >   We can use one of these circuits for each input to a Flip-
          Flop, and use a common control signal to determine
          direction.






A, B'     Output
1         A
0         B









                                                                                14

       SELECTING THE OUTPUT WITH GATES
       >   We also need to determine which direction output from a
           Flip Flop flows!
       >   This circuit has a common input, and selectable output.
       >   We can use one of these for each output from a Flip-Flop,
           and use a common control signal to determine direction.

A, B'     Input

1         A
0         B








                                                                                 15

LET'S PUT THIS ALL TOGETHER
 (JUST 2 FFS TO START WITH)




























                                                              16

ADD THE GATES FOR SETTING INPUT






























                                                            17

SELECT INPUT






























                                                                18

               ADD THE OUTPUT DIRECTION SELECTION
               >   R circuit enabled (red)




                                       in                                 out

                                       in                                 out


Input             D
 Q             D










                                                                                             19

               OTHER DIRECTION SELECTED?







                                        in                                   out

                                        in                                   out


Input             D
 Q             D










                                                                                                 20

             WHAT COULD POSSIBLY GO WRONG?
             >   Can't short the outputs of two gates together.
             >   Have to Add with an OR gate.



                                   in                             out

                                   in                             out

Conflict












                                                                                    21

         THE FIX
         >   The OR gates combine the outputs of the controlling AND
             gates and pass through the signal from whichever one is
             enabled... to the D inputs on each Flip-Flop.



                                 in       ff 1  ff 2                out

                                 in                                 out


 Generally need
about 3 gates per
  flip-flop per bit





                                                                                       22

NEED MORE DEPTH?
>  This is a 4-deep shift register (
   http://www.ee.usyd.edu.au/tutorials/digital_tutorial/part2/
   register06.html).
>  As you can see, there are alternative ways of wiring it up,
   with different logic gates.

















                                                                             23

TO MAKE A STACK...

>   So far we have made a 2-stage bi-directional 1-bit shift
    register.
>   To make a proper stack:
     1.   Add depth (flip-flops with associated control logic)
     2.   Add width (bits) in parallel (common clock, control
          signals).
          •   identical shift-registers - one for each bit.










                                                                              24

SUMMARY

>   Hardware stacks formed using banks of shift registers
>   Simple input selective circuits allow direction selectability:
     –  Programmable gates !
>   To make a proper stack:
     1.   Add depth (flip-flops with associated control logic)
     2.   Add width (bits) in parallel (common clock, control
          signals).
         •    identical shift-registers - one for each bit.






                                                                             25
